fields.name: osconfig_projects_patchJobs_get
fields.description: Get the patch job. This can be used to track the progress of an ongoing patch job or review the details of completed jobs.

args.name: pageToken

This can only be `true` when reading across collections. For example, when `parent` is set to `"projects/example/locations/-"`. This field is not supported by default and will result in an `UNIMPLEMENTED` error if set unless explicitly documented otherwise in service or product specific documentation.

fields.name: osconfig_projects_locations_instances_inventories_get

args.name: view

args.name: pageToken

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of results to return.

args.description: If provided, this field specifies the criteria that must be met by a `Inventory` API resource to be included in the response.

fields.name: osconfig_projects_locations_instances_vulnerabilityReports_get
fields.description: Gets the vulnerability report for the specified VM instance. Only VMs with inventory data have vulnerability reports associated with them.

fields.name: osconfig_projects_locations_instances_vulnerabilityReports_list
fields.description: List vulnerability reports for all VM instances in the specified zone.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: A pagination token returned from a previous call to `ListVulnerabilityReports` that indicates where this listing should continue from.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of results to return.

args.name: filter
args.description: This field supports filtering by the severity level for the vulnerability. For a list of severity levels, see [Severity levels for vulnerabilities](https://cloud.google.com/container-analysis/docs/container-scanning-overview#severity_levels_for_vulnerabilities). The filter field follows the rules described in the [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160) guidelines as follows: + **Filter for a specific severity type**: you can list reports that contain vulnerabilities that are classified as medium by specifying `vulnerabilities.details.severity:MEDIUM`. + **Filter for a range of severities** : you can list reports that have vulnerabilities that are classified as critical or high by specifying `vulnerabilities.details.severity:HIGH OR vulnerabilities.details.severity:CRITICAL`

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: PatchJob
types.description: A high level representation of a patch job that is either in progress or has completed. Instance details are not included in the job. To paginate through instance details, use `ListPatchJobInstanceDetails`. For more information about patch jobs, see [Creating patch jobs](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/os-patch-management/create-patch-job).

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Display name for this patch job. This is not a unique identifier.

fields.name: patchDeployment
fields.description: Output only. Name of the patch deployment that created this patch job.

fields.name: dryRun
fields.description: If this patch job is a dry run, the agent reports that it has finished without running any updates on the VM instance.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Time this patch job was created.

fields.name: errorMessage
fields.description: If this patch job failed, this message provides information about the failure.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the patch job. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Last time this patch job was updated.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Duration of the patch job. After the duration ends, the patch job times out.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Unique identifier for this patch job in the form `projects/*/patchJobs/*`

fields.name: percentComplete
fields.description: Reflects the overall progress of the patch job in the range of 0.0 being no progress to 100.0 being complete.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: PatchConfig
types.description: Patch configuration specifications. Contains details on how to apply the patch(es) to a VM instance.

fields.name: migInstancesAllowed
fields.description: Allows the patch job to run on Managed instance groups (MIGs).

fields.name: goo
fields.description: Googet patching is performed by running `googet update`.

fields.name: skipUnpatchableVms
fields.description: Optional. Enables enhanced reporting for the patch job: 1. The patch job skips instances that cannot be patched and reports them as `SKIPPED`. An instance cannot be patched for two reasons: 1. The instance runs Container-Optimized OS (COS), which cannot be patched. 2. The instance is part of a managed instance group (MIG), and patching MIG instances is disabled in the patch job's configuration (PatchConfig.migInstancesAllowed is `false`). 2. The patch job is reported as `SUCCEEDED` if it completes without errors, even if some instances are `SKIPPED`. 3. The patch job is reported as `COMPLETED_WITH_INACTIVE_VMS` if it completes without errors, but does not patch instances that are `INACTIVE`.

types.name: WindowsUpdateSettings
types.description: Windows patching is performed using the Windows Update Agent.

fields.name: classifications
fields.description: Only apply updates of these windows update classifications. If empty, all updates are applied.

fields.name: excludes
fields.description: List of KBs to exclude from update.

fields.name: exclusivePatches
fields.description: An exclusive list of kbs to be updated. These are the only patches that will be updated. This field must not be used with other patch configurations.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchJobs_execute_patchConfig_rebootConfig
types.description: Post-patch reboot settings.

types.name: ExecStep
types.description: A step that runs an executable for a PatchJob.

types.name: ExecStepConfig
types.description: Common configurations for an ExecStep.

fields.name: allowedSuccessCodes
fields.description: Defaults to [0]. A list of possible return values that the execution can return to indicate a success.

fields.name: localPath
fields.description: An absolute path to the executable on the VM.

types.name: GcsObject
types.description: Cloud Storage object representation.

fields.name: bucket
fields.description: Required. Bucket of the Cloud Storage object.

fields.name: object
fields.description: Required. Name of the Cloud Storage object.

fields.name: generationNumber
fields.description: Required. Generation number of the Cloud Storage object. This is used to ensure that the ExecStep specified by this PatchJob does not change.

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchJobs_execute_patchConfig_postStep_windowsExecStepConfig_interpreter
types.description: The script interpreter to use to run the script. If no interpreter is specified the script will be executed directly, which will likely only succeed for scripts with [shebang lines] (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shebang_\(Unix\)).

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: ZypperSettings
types.description: Zypper patching is performed by running `zypper patch`. See also https://en.opensuse.org/SDB:Zypper_manual.

fields.name: withOptional
fields.description: Adds the `--with-optional` flag to `zypper patch`.

fields.name: withUpdate
fields.description: Adds the `--with-update` flag, to `zypper patch`.

fields.name: severities
fields.description: Install only patches with these severities. Common severities include critical, important, moderate, and low.

fields.name: categories
fields.description: Install only patches with these categories. Common categories include security, recommended, and feature.

fields.name: excludes
fields.description: List of patches to exclude from update.

fields.name: exclusivePatches
fields.description: An exclusive list of patches to be updated. These are the only patches that will be installed using 'zypper patch patch:' command. This field must not be used with any other patch configuration fields.

types.name: YumSettings
types.description: Yum patching is performed by executing `yum update`. Additional options can be set to control how this is executed. Note that not all settings are supported on all platforms.

fields.name: excludes
fields.description: List of packages to exclude from update. These packages are excluded by using the yum `--exclude` flag.

fields.name: exclusivePackages
fields.description: An exclusive list of packages to be updated. These are the only packages that will be updated. If these packages are not installed, they will be ignored. This field must not be specified with any other patch configuration fields.

fields.name: minimal
fields.description: Will cause patch to run `yum update-minimal` instead.

fields.name: security
fields.description: Adds the `--security` flag to `yum update`. Not supported on all platforms.

types.name: AptSettings
types.description: Apt patching is completed by executing `apt-get update && apt-get upgrade`. Additional options can be set to control how this is executed.

fields.name: exclusivePackages
fields.description: An exclusive list of packages to be updated. These are the only packages that will be updated. If these packages are not installed, they will be ignored. This field cannot be specified with any other patch configuration fields.

fields.name: excludes
fields.description: List of packages to exclude from update. These packages will be excluded

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchJobs_execute_patchConfig_apt_type
types.description: By changing the type to DIST, the patching is performed using `apt-get dist-upgrade` instead.

types.name: PatchJobInstanceDetailsSummary
types.description: A summary of the current patch state across all instances that this patch job affects. Contains counts of instances in different states. These states map to `InstancePatchState`. List patch job instance details to see the specific states of each instance.

fields.name: pendingInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances pending patch job.

fields.name: prePatchStepInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that are running the pre-patch step.

fields.name: skippedInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that were skipped during patching.

fields.name: notifiedInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances notified about patch job.

fields.name: timedOutInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that exceeded the time out while applying the patch.

fields.name: applyingPatchesInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that are applying patches.

fields.name: noAgentDetectedInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that do not appear to be running the agent. Check to ensure that the agent is installed, running, and able to communicate with the service.

fields.name: failedInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that failed.

fields.name: ackedInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that have acked and will start shortly.

fields.name: inactiveInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that are inactive.

fields.name: succeededInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that have completed successfully.

fields.name: succeededRebootRequiredInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that require reboot.

fields.name: downloadingPatchesInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that are downloading patches.

fields.name: startedInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that have started.

fields.name: rebootingInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances rebooting.

fields.name: postPatchStepInstanceCount
fields.description: Number of instances that are running the post-patch step.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchJobs_execute_state
types.description: The current state of the PatchJob.

types.name: PatchInstanceFilter
types.description: A filter to target VM instances for patching. The targeted VMs must meet all criteria specified. So if both labels and zones are specified, the patch job targets only VMs with those labels and in those zones.

fields.name: instanceNamePrefixes
fields.description: Targets VMs whose name starts with one of these prefixes. Similar to labels, this is another way to group VMs when targeting configs, for example prefix="prod-".

fields.name: instances
fields.description: Targets any of the VM instances specified. Instances are specified by their URI in the form `zones/[ZONE]/instances/[INSTANCE_NAME]`, `projects/[PROJECT_ID]/zones/[ZONE]/instances/[INSTANCE_NAME]`, or `https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/[PROJECT_ID]/zones/[ZONE]/instances/[INSTANCE_NAME]`

fields.name: zones
fields.description: Targets VM instances in ANY of these zones. Leave empty to target VM instances in any zone.

fields.name: all
fields.description: Target all VM instances in the project. If true, no other criteria is permitted.

fields.name: groupLabels
fields.description: Targets VM instances matching ANY of these GroupLabels. This allows targeting of disparate groups of VM instances.

types.name: PatchInstanceFilterGroupLabel
types.description: Targets a group of VM instances by using their [assigned labels](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/labeling-resources). Labels are key-value pairs. A `GroupLabel` is a combination of labels that is used to target VMs for a patch job. For example, a patch job can target VMs that have the following `GroupLabel`: `{"env":"test", "app":"web"}`. This means that the patch job is applied to VMs that have both the labels `env=test` and `app=web`.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Compute Engine instance labels that must be present for a VM instance to be targeted by this filter.

types.name: PatchRollout
types.description: Patch rollout configuration specifications. Contains details on the concurrency control when applying patch(es) to all targeted VMs.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchJobs_execute_rollout_mode
types.description: Mode of the patch rollout.

types.name: FixedOrPercent
types.description: Message encapsulating a value that can be either absolute ("fixed") or relative ("percent") to a value.

fields.name: fixed
fields.description: Specifies a fixed value.

fields.name: percent
fields.description: Specifies the relative value defined as a percentage, which will be multiplied by a reference value.

fields.name: attemptCount

types.name: PatchDeployment
types.description: Patch deployments are configurations that individual patch jobs use to complete a patch. These configurations include instance filter, package repository settings, and a schedule. For more information about creating and managing patch deployments, see [Scheduling patch jobs](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/os-patch-management/schedule-patch-jobs).

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Optional. Duration of the patch. After the duration ends, the patch times out.

fields.name: lastExecuteTime
fields.description: Output only. The last time a patch job was started by this deployment. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Unique name for the patch deployment resource in a project. The patch deployment name is in the form: `projects/{project_id}/patchDeployments/{patch_deployment_id}`. This field is ignored when you create a new patch deployment.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Time the patch deployment was created. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional. Description of the patch deployment. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Time the patch deployment was last updated. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchDeployments_create_state
types.description: Output only. Current state of the patch deployment.

types.name: OneTimeSchedule
types.description: Sets the time for a one time patch deployment. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

fields.name: executeTime
fields.description: Required. The desired patch job execution time.

types.name: RecurringSchedule
types.description: Sets the time for recurring patch deployments.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Optional. The end time at which a recurring patch deployment schedule is no longer active.

fields.name: lastExecuteTime
fields.description: Output only. The time the last patch job ran successfully.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Optional. The time that the recurring schedule becomes effective. Defaults to `create_time` of the patch deployment.

fields.name: nextExecuteTime
fields.description: Output only. The time the next patch job is scheduled to run.

types.name: TimeZone
types.description: Represents a time zone from the [IANA Time Zone Database](https://www.iana.org/time-zones).

fields.name: id
fields.description: IANA Time Zone Database time zone. For example "America/New_York".

fields.name: version
fields.description: Optional. IANA Time Zone Database version number. For example "2019a".

types.name: TimeOfDay
types.description: Represents a time of day. The date and time zone are either not significant or are specified elsewhere. An API may choose to allow leap seconds. Related types are google.type.Date and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

fields.name: seconds
fields.description: Seconds of a minute. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must be less than or equal to 59. An API may allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

fields.name: hours
fields.description: Hours of a day in 24 hour format. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must be less than or equal to 23. An API may choose to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

fields.name: minutes
fields.description: Minutes of an hour. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 59.

fields.name: nanos
fields.description: Fractions of seconds, in nanoseconds. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 999,999,999.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchDeployments_create_recurringSchedule_frequency
types.description: Required. The frequency unit of this recurring schedule.

types.name: WeeklySchedule
types.description: Represents a weekly schedule.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchDeployments_create_recurringSchedule_weekly_dayOfWeek
types.description: Required. Day of the week.

types.name: MonthlySchedule
types.description: Represents a monthly schedule. An example of a valid monthly schedule is "on the third Tuesday of the month" or "on the 15th of the month".

fields.name: monthDay
fields.description: Required. One day of the month. 1-31 indicates the 1st to the 31st day. -1 indicates the last day of the month. Months without the target day will be skipped. For example, a schedule to run "every month on the 31st" will not run in February, April, June, etc.

types.name: WeekDayOfMonth
types.description: Represents one week day in a month. An example is "the 4th Sunday".

fields.name: dayOffset
fields.description: Optional. Represents the number of days before or after the given week day of month that the patch deployment is scheduled for. For example if `week_ordinal` and `day_of_week` values point to the second Tuesday of the month and the `day_offset` value is set to `3`, patch deployment takes place three days after the second Tuesday of the month. If this value is negative, for example -5, patches are deployed five days before the second Tuesday of the month. Allowed values are in range [-30, 30].

fields.name: weekOrdinal
fields.description: Required. Week number in a month. 1-4 indicates the 1st to 4th week of the month. -1 indicates the last week of the month.

types.name: mutation_osconfig_projects_patchDeployments_create_recurringSchedule_monthly_weekDayOfMonth_dayOfWeek
types.description: Required. A day of the week.

types.name: Operation
types.description: This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a network API call.

fields.description: The normal, successful response of the operation. If the original method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is `google.protobuf.Empty`. If the original method is standard `Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource. For other methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx` is the original method name. For example, if the original method name is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is `TakeSnapshotResponse`.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Service-specific metadata associated with the operation. It typically contains progress information and common metadata such as create time. Some services might not provide such metadata. Any method that returns a long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

fields.name: done
fields.description: If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress. If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is available.

types.name: Status
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

fields.name: details

fields.name: code

fields.name: message
fields.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

types.name: ID

fields.description: The OS policy id

fields.name: complianceStateReason
fields.description: The reason for the OS policy to be in an unknown compliance state. This field is always populated when `compliance_state` is `UNKNOWN`. If populated, the field can contain one of the following values: * `vm-not-running`: The VM was not running. * `os-policies-not-supported-by-agent`: The version of the OS Config agent running on the VM does not support running OS policies. * `no-agent-detected`: The OS Config agent is not detected for the VM. * `resource-execution-errors`: The OS Config agent encountered errors while executing one or more resources in the policy. See `os_policy_resource_compliances` for details. * `task-timeout`: The task sent to the agent to apply the policy timed out. * `unexpected-agent-state`: The OS Config agent did not report the final status of the task that attempted to apply the policy. Instead, the agent unexpectedly started working on a different task. This mostly happens when the agent or VM unexpectedly restarts while applying OS policies. * `internal-service-errors`: Internal service errors were encountered while attempting to apply the policy. * `os-policy-execution-pending`: OS policy was assigned to the given VM, but was not executed yet. Typically this is a transient condition that will go away after the next policy execution cycle.

fields.name: osPolicyResourceCompliances

The following values are supported when `compliance_state == UNKNOWN` * `execution-errors`: Errors were encountered by the agent while executing the resource and the compliance state couldn't be determined. * `execution-skipped-by-agent`: Resource execution was skipped by the agent because errors were encountered while executing prior resources in the OS policy. * `os-policy-execution-attempt-failed`: The execution of the OS policy containing this resource failed and the compliance state couldn't be determined. * `os-policy-execution-pending`: OS policy that owns this resource was assigned to the given VM, but was not executed yet.

types.name: OSPolicyAssignment
types.description: OS policy assignment is an API resource that is used to apply a set of OS policies to a dynamically targeted group of Compute Engine VM instances. An OS policy is used to define the desired state configuration for a Compute Engine VM instance through a set of configuration resources that provide capabilities such as installing or removing software packages, or executing a script. For more information about the OS policy resource definitions and examples, see [OS policy and OS policy assignment](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/os-configuration-management/working-with-os-policies).

fields.name: reconciling
fields.description: Output only. Indicates that reconciliation is in progress for the revision. This value is `true` when the `rollout_state` is one of: * IN_PROGRESS * CANCELLING

fields.name: name
fields.description: Resource name. Format: `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location}/osPolicyAssignments/{os_policy_assignment_id}` This field is ignored when you create an OS policy assignment.

fields.name: baseline
fields.description: Output only. Indicates that this revision has been successfully rolled out in this zone and new VMs will be assigned OS policies from this revision. For a given OS policy assignment, there is only one revision with a value of `true` for this field.

fields.name: uid
fields.description: Output only. Server generated unique id for the OS policy assignment resource.

fields.name: deleted
fields.description: Output only. Indicates that this revision deletes the OS policy assignment.

fields.name: osPolicies
fields.description: Required. List of OS policies to be applied to the VMs.

fields.name: description
fields.description: OS policy assignment description. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

fields.name: revisionId
fields.description: Output only. The assignment revision ID A new revision is committed whenever a rollout is triggered for a OS policy assignment

fields.name: revisionCreateTime
fields.description: Output only. The timestamp that the revision was created.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: The etag for this OS policy assignment. If this is provided on update, it must match the server's etag.

fields.name: minWaitDuration
fields.description: Required. This determines the minimum duration of time to wait after the configuration changes are applied through the current rollout. A VM continues to count towards the `disruption_budget` at least until this duration of time has passed after configuration changes are applied.

types.description: Filters to select target VMs for an assignment. If more than one filter criteria is specified below, a VM will be selected if and only if it satisfies all of them.

fields.name: inclusionLabels
fields.description: List of label sets used for VM inclusion. If the list has more than one `LabelSet`, the VM is included if any of the label sets are applicable for the VM.

fields.name: exclusionLabels
fields.description: List of label sets used for VM exclusion. If the list has more than one label set, the VM is excluded if any of the label sets are applicable for the VM.

fields.name: all
fields.description: Target all VMs in the project. If true, no other criteria is permitted.

fields.name: inventories
fields.description: List of inventories to select VMs. A VM is selected if its inventory data matches at least one of the following inventories.

types.name: OSPolicyAssignmentLabelSet
types.description: Message representing label set. * A label is a key value pair set for a VM. * A LabelSet is a set of labels. * Labels within a LabelSet are ANDed. In other words, a LabelSet is applicable for a VM only if it matches all the labels in the LabelSet. * Example: A LabelSet with 2 labels: `env=prod` and `type=webserver` will only be applicable for those VMs with both labels present.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Labels are identified by key/value pairs in this map. A VM should contain all the key/value pairs specified in this map to be selected.

types.description: VM inventory details.

types.name: OSPolicy
types.description: An OS policy defines the desired state configuration for a VM.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Policy description. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

fields.name: resourceGroups
fields.description: Required. List of resource groups for the policy. For a particular VM, resource groups are evaluated in the order specified and the first resource group that is applicable is selected and the rest are ignored. If none of the resource groups are applicable for a VM, the VM is considered to be non-compliant w.r.t this policy. This behavior can be toggled by the flag `allow_no_resource_group_match`

fields.name: id
fields.description: Required. The id of the OS policy with the following restrictions: * Must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. * Must start with a letter. * Must be between 1-63 characters. * Must end with a number or a letter. * Must be unique within the assignment.

fields.name: allowNoResourceGroupMatch
fields.description: This flag determines the OS policy compliance status when none of the resource groups within the policy are applicable for a VM. Set this value to `true` if the policy needs to be reported as compliant even if the policy has nothing to validate or enforce.

types.name: OSPolicyResourceGroup
types.description: Resource groups provide a mechanism to group OS policy resources. Resource groups enable OS policy authors to create a single OS policy to be applied to VMs running different operating Systems. When the OS policy is applied to a target VM, the appropriate resource group within the OS policy is selected based on the `OSFilter` specified within the resource group.

fields.name: inventoryFilters
fields.description: List of inventory filters for the resource group. The resources in this resource group are applied to the target VM if it satisfies at least one of the following inventory filters. For example, to apply this resource group to VMs running either `RHEL` or `CentOS` operating systems, specify 2 items for the list with following values: inventory_filters[0].os_short_name='rhel' and inventory_filters[1].os_short_name='centos' If the list is empty, this resource group will be applied to the target VM unconditionally.

fields.name: resources
fields.description: Required. List of resources configured for this resource group. The resources are executed in the exact order specified here.

fields.name: permissions
fields.description: Consists of three octal digits which represent, in order, the permissions of the owner, group, and other users for the file (similarly to the numeric mode used in the linux chmod utility). Each digit represents a three bit number with the 4 bit corresponding to the read permissions, the 2 bit corresponds to the write bit, and the one bit corresponds to the execute permission. Default behavior is 755. Below are some examples of permissions and their associated values: read, write, and execute: 7 read and execute: 5 read and write: 6 read only: 4

types.name: OSPolicyResourceExecResource
types.description: A resource that allows executing scripts on the VM. The `ExecResource` has 2 stages: `validate` and `enforce` and both stages accept a script as an argument to execute. When the `ExecResource` is applied by the agent, it first executes the script in the `validate` stage. The `validate` stage can signal that the `ExecResource` is already in the desired state by returning an exit code of `100`. If the `ExecResource` is not in the desired state, it should return an exit code of `101`. Any other exit code returned by this stage is considered an error. If the `ExecResource` is not in the desired state based on the exit code from the `validate` stage, the agent proceeds to execute the script from the `enforce` stage. If the `ExecResource` is already in the desired state, the `enforce` stage will not be run. Similar to `validate` stage, the `enforce` stage should return an exit code of `100` to indicate that the resource in now in its desired state. Any other exit code is considered an error. NOTE: An exit code of `100` was chosen over `0` (and `101` vs `1`) to have an explicit indicator of `in desired state`, `not in desired state` and errors. Because, for example, Powershell will always return an exit code of `0` unless an `exit` statement is provided in the script. So, for reasons of consistency and being explicit, exit codes `100` and `101` were chosen.

Absence or failure to read this file will result in this ExecResource being non-compliant. Output file size is limited to 500K bytes.

fields.name: osconfig_projects_locations_operations_cancel
fields.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of `1`, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

If the LRO completes and is not cancelled, all revisions associated with the OS policy assignment are deleted. types.name: ExecutePatchJobRequest_Input
types.description: A request message to initiate patching across Compute Engine instances.

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: Display name for this patch job. This does not have to be unique.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Description of the patch job. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

inputFields.name: duration
inputFields.description: Duration of the patch job. After the duration ends, the patch job times out.

inputFields.name: dryRun
inputFields.description: If this patch is a dry-run only, instances are contacted but will do nothing.

types.name: PatchConfig_Input
types.description: Patch configuration specifications. Contains details on how to apply the patch(es) to a VM instance.

inputFields.name: migInstancesAllowed
inputFields.description: Allows the patch job to run on Managed instance groups (MIGs).

inputFields.name: goo
inputFields.description: Googet patching is performed by running `googet update`.

inputFields.name: skipUnpatchableVms
inputFields.description: Optional. Enables enhanced reporting for the patch job: 1. The patch job skips instances that cannot be patched and reports them as `SKIPPED`. An instance cannot be patched for two reasons: 1. The instance runs Container-Optimized OS (COS), which cannot be patched. 2. The instance is part of a managed instance group (MIG), and patching MIG instances is disabled in the patch job's configuration (PatchConfig.migInstancesAllowed is `false`). 2. The patch job is reported as `SUCCEEDED` if it completes without errors, even if some instances are `SKIPPED`. 3. The patch job is reported as `COMPLETED_WITH_INACTIVE_VMS` if it completes without errors, but does not patch instances that are `INACTIVE`.

types.name: WindowsUpdateSettings_Input
types.description: Windows patching is performed using the Windows Update Agent.

inputFields.name: classifications
inputFields.description: Only apply updates of these windows update classifications. If empty, all updates are applied.

inputFields.name: excludes
inputFields.description: List of KBs to exclude from update.

inputFields.name: exclusivePatches
inputFields.description: An exclusive list of kbs to be updated. These are the only patches that will be updated. This field must not be used with other patch configurations.

types.name: ExecStep_Input
types.description: A step that runs an executable for a PatchJob.

types.name: ExecStepConfig_Input
types.description: Common configurations for an ExecStep.

inputFields.name: allowedSuccessCodes
inputFields.description: Defaults to [0]. A list of possible return values that the execution can return to indicate a success.

inputFields.name: localPath
inputFields.description: An absolute path to the executable on the VM.

types.name: GcsObject_Input

types.name: ZypperSettings_Input
types.description: Zypper patching is performed by running `zypper patch`. See also https://en.opensuse.org/SDB:Zypper_manual.

inputFields.name: withOptional
inputFields.description: Adds the `--with-optional` flag to `zypper patch`.

inputFields.name: withUpdate
inputFields.description: Adds the `--with-update` flag, to `zypper patch`.

inputFields.name: severities
inputFields.description: Install only patches with these severities. Common severities include critical, important, moderate, and low.

inputFields.name: categories
inputFields.description: Install only patches with these categories. Common categories include security, recommended, and feature.

inputFields.name: excludes
inputFields.description: List of patches to exclude from update.

inputFields.name: exclusivePatches
inputFields.description: An exclusive list of patches to be updated. These are the only patches that will be installed using 'zypper patch patch:' command. This field must not be used with any other patch configuration fields.

types.name: YumSettings_Input
types.description: Yum patching is performed by executing `yum update`. Additional options can be set to control how this is executed. Note that not all settings are supported on all platforms.

inputFields.name: excludes
inputFields.description: List of packages to exclude from update. These packages are excluded by using the yum `--exclude` flag.

inputFields.name: exclusivePackages
inputFields.description: An exclusive list of packages to be updated. These are the only packages that will be updated. If these packages are not installed, they will be ignored. This field must not be specified with any other patch configuration fields.

inputFields.name: minimal
inputFields.description: Will cause patch to run `yum update-minimal` instead.

inputFields.name: security
inputFields.description: Adds the `--security` flag to `yum update`. Not supported on all platforms.

types.name: AptSettings_Input
types.description: Apt patching is completed by executing `apt-get update && apt-get upgrade`. Additional options can be set to control how this is executed.

inputFields.name: exclusivePackages
inputFields.description: An exclusive list of packages to be updated. These are the only packages that will be updated. If these packages are not installed, they will be ignored. This field cannot be specified with any other patch configuration fields.

inputFields.name: excludes
inputFields.description: List of packages to exclude from update. These packages will be excluded

types.name: PatchRollout_Input
types.description: Patch rollout configuration specifications. Contains details on the concurrency control when applying patch(es) to all targeted VMs.

types.name: FixedOrPercent_Input
types.description: Message encapsulating a value that can be either absolute ("fixed") or relative ("percent") to a value.

inputFields.name: fixed
inputFields.description: Specifies a fixed value.

inputFields.name: percent
inputFields.description: Specifies the relative value defined as a percentage, which will be multiplied by a reference value.

types.name: PatchInstanceFilter_Input
types.description: A filter to target VM instances for patching. The targeted VMs must meet all criteria specified. So if both labels and zones are specified, the patch job targets only VMs with those labels and in those zones.

inputFields.name: instanceNamePrefixes
inputFields.description: Targets VMs whose name starts with one of these prefixes. Similar to labels, this is another way to group VMs when targeting configs, for example prefix="prod-".

inputFields.name: instances
inputFields.description: Targets any of the VM instances specified. Instances are specified by their URI in the form `zones/[ZONE]/instances/[INSTANCE_NAME]`, `projects/[PROJECT_ID]/zones/[ZONE]/instances/[INSTANCE_NAME]`, or `https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/[PROJECT_ID]/zones/[ZONE]/instances/[INSTANCE_NAME]`

inputFields.name: zones
inputFields.description: Targets VM instances in ANY of these zones. Leave empty to target VM instances in any zone.

inputFields.name: all
inputFields.description: Target all VM instances in the project. If true, no other criteria is permitted.

inputFields.name: groupLabels
inputFields.description: Targets VM instances matching ANY of these GroupLabels. This allows targeting of disparate groups of VM instances.

types.name: PatchInstanceFilterGroupLabel_Input
types.description: Targets a group of VM instances by using their [assigned labels](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/labeling-resources). Labels are key-value pairs. A `GroupLabel` is a combination of labels that is used to target VMs for a patch job. For example, a patch job can target VMs that have the following `GroupLabel`: `{"env":"test", "app":"web"}`. This means that the patch job is applied to VMs that have both the labels `env=test` and `app=web`.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Compute Engine instance labels that must be present for a VM instance to be targeted by this filter.

types.name: PatchDeployment_Input
types.description: Patch deployments are configurations that individual patch jobs use to complete a patch. These configurations include instance filter, package repository settings, and a schedule. For more information about creating and managing patch deployments, see [Scheduling patch jobs](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/os-patch-management/schedule-patch-jobs).

inputFields.name: duration
inputFields.description: Optional. Duration of the patch. After the duration ends, the patch times out.

inputFields.name: lastExecuteTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The last time a patch job was started by this deployment. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Unique name for the patch deployment resource in a project. The patch deployment name is in the form: `projects/{project_id}/patchDeployments/{patch_deployment_id}`. This field is ignored when you create a new patch deployment.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: Output only. Time the patch deployment was created. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. Description of the patch deployment. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

inputFields.name: updateTime
inputFields.description: Output only. Time the patch deployment was last updated. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

types.name: OneTimeSchedule_Input
types.description: Sets the time for a one time patch deployment. Timestamp is in [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) text format.

inputFields.name: executeTime
inputFields.description: Required. The desired patch job execution time.

types.name: RecurringSchedule_Input
types.description: Sets the time for recurring patch deployments.

inputFields.name: endTime
inputFields.description: Optional. The end time at which a recurring patch deployment schedule is no longer active.

inputFields.name: lastExecuteTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time the last patch job ran successfully.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: Optional. The time that the recurring schedule becomes effective. Defaults to `create_time` of the patch deployment.

inputFields.name: nextExecuteTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time the next patch job is scheduled to run.

types.name: TimeZone_Input
types.description: Represents a time zone from the [IANA Time Zone Database](https://www.iana.org/time-zones).

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: IANA Time Zone Database time zone. For example "America/New_York".

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: Optional. IANA Time Zone Database version number. For example "2019a".

types.name: TimeOfDay_Input
types.description: Represents a time of day. The date and time zone are either not significant or are specified elsewhere. An API may choose to allow leap seconds. Related types are google.type.Date and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

inputFields.name: seconds
inputFields.description: Seconds of a minute. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must be less than or equal to 59. An API may allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

inputFields.name: hours
inputFields.description: Hours of a day in 24 hour format. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must be less than or equal to 23. An API may choose to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

inputFields.name: minutes
inputFields.description: Minutes of an hour. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 59.

inputFields.name: nanos
inputFields.description: Fractions of seconds, in nanoseconds. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 999,999,999.

types.name: WeeklySchedule_Input
types.description: Represents a weekly schedule.

types.name: MonthlySchedule_Input
types.description: Represents a monthly schedule. An example of a valid monthly schedule is "on the third Tuesday of the month" or "on the 15th of the month".

inputFields.name: monthDay
inputFields.description: Required. One day of the month. 1-31 indicates the 1st to the 31st day. -1 indicates the last day of the month. Months without the target day will be skipped. For example, a schedule to run "every month on the 31st" will not run in February, April, June, etc.

types.name: WeekDayOfMonth_Input
types.description: Represents one week day in a month. An example is "the 4th Sunday".

inputFields.name: dayOffset
inputFields.description: Optional. Represents the number of days before or after the given week day of month that the patch deployment is scheduled for. For example if `week_ordinal` and `day_of_week` values point to the second Tuesday of the month and the `day_offset` value is set to `3`, patch deployment takes place three days after the second Tuesday of the month. If this value is negative, for example -5, patches are deployed five days before the second Tuesday of the month. Allowed values are in range [-30, 30].

inputFields.name: weekOrdinal
inputFields.description: Required. Week number in a month. 1-4 indicates the 1st to 4th week of the month. -1 indicates the last week of the month.

types.name: ProjectFeatureSettings_Input
types.description: ProjectFeatureSettings represents the VM Manager feature settings in a project. For more information, see Enable full VM Manager functionality.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. Immutable. Name specifies the URL for the ProjectFeatureSettings resource: projects/project_id/locations/global/projectFeatureSettings.

types.name: OSPolicyAssignment_Input
types.description: OS policy assignment is an API resource that is used to apply a set of OS policies to a dynamically targeted group of Compute Engine VM instances. An OS policy is used to define the desired state configuration for a Compute Engine VM instance through a set of configuration resources that provide capabilities such as installing or removing software packages, or executing a script. For more information about the OS policy resource definitions and examples, see [OS policy and OS policy assignment](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/os-configuration-management/working-with-os-policies).

inputFields.name: reconciling
inputFields.description: Output only. Indicates that reconciliation is in progress for the revision. This value is `true` when the `rollout_state` is one of: * IN_PROGRESS * CANCELLING

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Resource name. Format: `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location}/osPolicyAssignments/{os_policy_assignment_id}` This field is ignored when you create an OS policy assignment.

inputFields.name: baseline
inputFields.description: Output only. Indicates that this revision has been successfully rolled out in this zone and new VMs will be assigned OS policies from this revision. For a given OS policy assignment, there is only one revision with a value of `true` for this field.

inputFields.name: uid
inputFields.description: Output only. Server generated unique id for the OS policy assignment resource.

inputFields.name: deleted
inputFields.description: Output only. Indicates that this revision deletes the OS policy assignment.

inputFields.name: osPolicies
inputFields.description: Required. List of OS policies to be applied to the VMs.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: OS policy assignment description. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

inputFields.name: revisionId
inputFields.description: Output only. The assignment revision ID A new revision is committed whenever a rollout is triggered for a OS policy assignment

inputFields.name: revisionCreateTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The timestamp that the revision was created.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: The etag for this OS policy assignment. If this is provided on update, it must match the server's etag.

types.name: OSPolicyAssignmentRollout_Input
types.description: Message to configure the rollout at the zonal level for the OS policy assignment.

inputFields.name: minWaitDuration
inputFields.description: Required. This determines the minimum duration of time to wait after the configuration changes are applied through the current rollout. A VM continues to count towards the `disruption_budget` at least until this duration of time has passed after configuration changes are applied.

types.name: OSPolicyAssignmentInstanceFilter_Input
types.description: Filters to select target VMs for an assignment. If more than one filter criteria is specified below, a VM will be selected if and only if it satisfies all of them.

inputFields.name: inclusionLabels
inputFields.description: List of label sets used for VM inclusion. If the list has more than one `LabelSet`, the VM is included if any of the label sets are applicable for the VM.

inputFields.name: exclusionLabels
inputFields.description: List of label sets used for VM exclusion. If the list has more than one label set, the VM is excluded if any of the label sets are applicable for the VM.

inputFields.name: all
inputFields.description: Target all VMs in the project. If true, no other criteria is permitted.

inputFields.name: inventories
inputFields.description: List of inventories to select VMs. A VM is selected if its inventory data matches at least one of the following inventories.

types.name: OSPolicyAssignmentLabelSet_Input
types.description: Message representing label set. * A label is a key value pair set for a VM. * A LabelSet is a set of labels. * Labels within a LabelSet are ANDed. In other words, a LabelSet is applicable for a VM only if it matches all the labels in the LabelSet. * Example: A LabelSet with 2 labels: `env=prod` and `type=webserver` will only be applicable for those VMs with both labels present.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Labels are identified by key/value pairs in this map. A VM should contain all the key/value pairs specified in this map to be selected.

types.name: OSPolicyAssignmentInstanceFilterInventory_Input
types.description: VM inventory details.

inputFields.name: osShortName
inputFields.description: Required. The OS short name

inputFields.name: osVersion
inputFields.description: The OS version Prefix matches are supported if asterisk(*) is provided as the last character. For example, to match all versions with a major version of `7`, specify the following value for this field `7.*` An empty string matches all OS versions.

types.name: OSPolicy_Input
types.description: An OS policy defines the desired state configuration for a VM.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Policy description. Length of the description is limited to 1024 characters.

inputFields.name: resourceGroups
inputFields.description: Required. List of resource groups for the policy. For a particular VM, resource groups are evaluated in the order specified and the first resource group that is applicable is selected and the rest are ignored. If none of the resource groups are applicable for a VM, the VM is considered to be non-compliant w.r.t this policy. This behavior can be toggled by the flag `allow_no_resource_group_match`

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: Required. The id of the OS policy with the following restrictions: * Must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. * Must start with a letter. * Must be between 1-63 characters. * Must end with a number or a letter. * Must be unique within the assignment.

inputFields.name: allowNoResourceGroupMatch
inputFields.description: This flag determines the OS policy compliance status when none of the resource groups within the policy are applicable for a VM. Set this value to `true` if the policy needs to be reported as compliant even if the policy has nothing to validate or enforce.

types.name: OSPolicyResourceGroup_Input
types.description: Resource groups provide a mechanism to group OS policy resources. Resource groups enable OS policy authors to create a single OS policy to be applied to VMs running different operating Systems. When the OS policy is applied to a target VM, the appropriate resource group within the OS policy is selected based on the `OSFilter` specified within the resource group.

inputFields.name: inventoryFilters
inputFields.description: List of inventory filters for the resource group. The resources in this resource group are applied to the target VM if it satisfies at least one of the following inventory filters. For example, to apply this resource group to VMs running either `RHEL` or `CentOS` operating systems, specify 2 items for the list with following values: inventory_filters[0].os_short_name='rhel' and inventory_filters[1].os_short_name='centos' If the list is empty, this resource group will be applied to the target VM unconditionally.

inputFields.name: resources
inputFields.description: Required. List of resources configured for this resource group. The resources are executed in the exact order specified here.

types.name: OSPolicyInventoryFilter_Input
types.description: Filtering criteria to select VMs based on inventory details.

inputFields.name: osVersion
inputFields.description: The OS version Prefix matches are supported if asterisk(*) is provided as the last character. For example, to match all versions with a major version of `7`, specify the following value for this field `7.*` An empty string matches all OS versions.

inputFields.name: osShortName
inputFields.description: Required. The OS short name

types.name: OSPolicyResource_Input
types.description: An OS policy resource is used to define the desired state configuration and provides a specific functionality like installing/removing packages, executing a script etc. The system ensures that resources are always in their desired state by taking necessary actions if they have drifted from their desired state.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: Required. The id of the resource with the following restrictions: * Must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. * Must start with a letter. * Must be between 1-63 characters. * Must end with a number or a letter. * Must be unique within the OS policy.

types.name: OSPolicyResourceFileResource_Input
types.description: A resource that manages the state of a file.

inputFields.name: path
inputFields.description: Required. The absolute path of the file within the VM.

inputFields.name: permissions
inputFields.description: Consists of three octal digits which represent, in order, the permissions of the owner, group, and other users for the file (similarly to the numeric mode used in the linux chmod utility). Each digit represents a three bit number with the 4 bit corresponding to the read permissions, the 2 bit corresponds to the write bit, and the one bit corresponds to the execute permission. Default behavior is 755. Below are some examples of permissions and their associated values: read, write, and execute: 7 read and execute: 5 read and write: 6 read only: 4

inputFields.name: content
inputFields.description: A a file with this content. The size of the content is limited to 32KiB.

types.name: OSPolicyResourceExecResource_Input
types.description: A resource that allows executing scripts on the VM. The `ExecResource` has 2 stages: `validate` and `enforce` and both stages accept a script as an argument to execute. When the `ExecResource` is applied by the agent, it first executes the script in the `validate` stage. The `validate` stage can signal that the `ExecResource` is already in the desired state by returning an exit code of `100`. If the `ExecResource` is not in the desired state, it should return an exit code of `101`. Any other exit code returned by this stage is considered an error. If the `ExecResource` is not in the desired state based on the exit code from the `validate` stage, the agent proceeds to execute the script from the `enforce` stage. If the `ExecResource` is already in the desired state, the `enforce` stage will not be run. Similar to `validate` stage, the `enforce` stage should return an exit code of `100` to indicate that the resource in now in its desired state. Any other exit code is considered an error. NOTE: An exit code of `100` was chosen over `0` (and `101` vs `1`) to have an explicit indicator of `in desired state`, `not in desired state` and errors. Because, for example, Powershell will always return an exit code of `0` unless an `exit` statement is provided in the script. So, for reasons of consistency and being explicit, exit codes `100` and `101` were chosen.

types.name: OSPolicyResourceExecResourceExec_Input
types.description: A file or script to execute.

inputFields.name: script
inputFields.description: An inline script. The size of the script is limited to 32KiB.

inputFields.name: outputFilePath
inputFields.description: Only recorded for enforce Exec. Path to an output file (that is created by this Exec) whose content will be recorded in OSPolicyResourceCompliance after a successful run. Absence or failure to read this file will result in this ExecResource being non-compliant. Output file size is limited to 500K bytes.

inputFields.name: args
inputFields.description: Optional arguments to pass to the source during execution.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
